title: Fixup Goal-Scoped Config, Autopilot, LLM Observability und Acceptance Runner

created_from_analysis.analysis_scope: Commits on main up to 5cbd64a0, especially goal-scoped config, provider observer, autopilot recovery, llm_call_profile, acceptance runner, generated artifacts and model defaults.
created_from_analysis.summary: The commit range introduced goal-scoped configuration snapshots, config profiles, provider observation, LLM call profiling, planning recovery, acceptance runner extensions and many generated runtime artifacts. This TODO turns the current implementation into a stable, deterministic, testable and cleaner production-ready feature set.

principles.text: Default behavior must be deterministic. Heuristic fallback is only allowed when explicitly configured and visibly reported.
No secret, token, password, API key or credential-like value may be persisted into goal snapshots, artifacts, logs, acceptance reports or UI responses in plaintext.
Real provider/LLM metrics and synthetic orchestration placeholders must be strictly separated in API, persistence, diagnostics and UI.
A goal must keep the config snapshot it was created with. Later global config changes must not silently alter running or historical goal behavior.
Runtime-generated project-workspaces, diagnostic reports and acceptance outputs must not pollute source control unless they are explicit fixtures.

tracks.title: Goal-Scoped Config absichern und deterministisch machen
tracks.rationale: Goal-scoped config is now central for worker/runtime/model selection. It must be deterministic, audited and safe against invalid overrides or secret leakage.

tasks.title: Allowed config keys zentral definieren
tasks.description: Move the allowed top-level keys for goal-scoped config into one reusable definition instead of keeping them only inside the resolver implementation.
implementation_notes: Create a central constant or schema object for allowed goal-scoped config keys.
Use it in resolver, validation, API docs and tests.
Unknown keys must be rejected or reported explicitly, not silently ignored unless compatibility mode is enabled.
acceptance_criteria: There is exactly one authoritative list/schema of allowed goal-scoped config keys.
Goal config resolver, API validation and tests consume the same source of truth.
Unknown override keys are visible in a validation/reporting result.
No existing valid profile breaks.
verification: Add a test with an unknown key and assert deterministic rejection/reporting.

tasks.title: Override-Validation vor Goal-Erstellung erzwingen
tasks.description: Before creating a goal, validate config_profile and config_overrides structurally and semantically.
implementation_notes: Validate override type, allowed keys and known profile id before persisting the goal.
Return stable error codes like invalid_config_overrides, unknown_config_profile, invalid_goal_config_key.
Validation must not depend on LLMs or external provider state.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid config_overrides never produce a persisted GoalDB record.
Unknown config_profile returns HTTP 400 with a deterministic error message.
Non-admin callers cannot override restricted runtime/security settings if such restrictions are configured.
Tests cover valid profile, invalid profile, invalid override type and unknown key.
verification: Manual API call: POST /goals with invalid config_overrides returns 400.

tasks.title: Config-Snapshot-Checksum stabilisieren
tasks.description: Make checksum generation stable and explicitly define whether it is calculated over redacted or unredacted snapshots.
implementation_notes: Define checksum contract: recommended is checksum over canonical redacted persisted snapshot to avoid secret-sensitive checksums.
Document this in code comments and tests.
Ensure volatile fields such as resolved_at do not accidentally change checksum unless intentionally included.
acceptance_criteria: Two identical configs produce identical checksums across processes.
Changing one effective config value changes the checksum.
Adding a secret value does not leak secret content through persisted snapshot.
Checksum behavior is covered by tests.

tasks.title: Secret-Redaction robuster machen
tasks.description: Current redaction is key-name based. Extend and test it for common credential field variants.
implementation_notes: Cover api_key, access_token, refresh_token, bearer, authorization, password, secret, credential, private_key.
Avoid redacting harmless fields like keyboard or token_count if false positives become a problem; define behavior explicitly.
Add recursive tests for nested dict/list structures.
acceptance_criteria: Nested secrets are redacted recursively.
Redaction summary reports the count of redacted fields.
No plaintext test secret appears in config_snapshot, effective-config endpoint response or acceptance report.
Tests include nested list/dict secret payloads.

tasks.title: Effective-Config Endpoint vollständig absichern
tasks.description: Ensure /goals/<goal_id>/effective-config respects the same access control as goal detail and never exposes unredacted config.
implementation_notes: Reuse _can_access_goal.
Add non-owner/non-admin access test if test helpers exist.
Return only redacted snapshot and provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthorized user gets 404 or 403 consistently with goal detail behavior.
Authorized user receives redacted config_snapshot only.
Endpoint includes goal_id, config_snapshot, provenance, checksum/hash, redaction_summary and goal_config_source.
Test verifies no secret-like value is present in the response.

tasks.title: Task-Level Config Override sauber anbinden
tasks.description: The resolver supports task_overrides conceptually. Wire it through task execution/propose paths or explicitly disable it until implemented.
implementation_notes: Locate all task execution/propose entry points where effective config is needed.
Resolve config as system_default + profile + goal + task.
Persist provenance for task-specific overrides if present.
If task_overrides are not supported yet, reject them deterministically.
acceptance_criteria: Task execution uses the same effective goal config as planning unless a valid task override exists.
Task override provenance is visible in diagnostics when used.
Invalid task overrides do not silently fall back.
Tests prove goal-only and goal+task override resolution.

tracks.title: Config-Profile und Runtime-Auswahl vervollständigen
tracks.rationale: Profiles currently exist as static presets. They need validation, UI/API discoverability and exact binding to worker/model runtime selection.

tasks.title: Config-Profiles API dokumentieren und testen
tasks.description: Expose available config profiles clearly to frontend/CLI and acceptance runner.
implementation_notes: Ensure profile id, description and safe overrides are returned.
Do not expose secret values.
Add tests for all default profiles.
acceptance_criteria: API returns opencode_preconfigured, opencode_ollama_local and ananta_ollama_local.
Each profile has id, description and overrides.
Returned overrides are redacted or guaranteed secret-free.
Tests fail if a profile references an unknown top-level config key.

tasks.title: Profile gegen Worker-Verfügbarkeit validieren
tasks.description: A selected profile should report whether required provider/worker backends are currently observable, without blocking deterministic goal creation unless policy requires it.
implementation_notes: Define validation levels: structural_valid, provider_observable, worker_observable.
Do not make provider availability a hidden heuristic decision.
Expose warnings separately from hard errors.
acceptance_criteria: Profile selection can report missing Ollama, LM Studio or worker backend as explicit warning/error.
Policy decides whether missing provider blocks goal creation.
Tests cover unavailable provider with allow and deny policy.

tasks.title: Runtime-Auswahl bis zum Worker durchreichen
tasks.description: Ensure selected provider/model/backend from goal-scoped config is actually used by propose and execute paths, not only stored as metadata.
implementation_notes: Trace config_snapshot -> model selection -> propose strategy -> worker invocation.
Add a runtime_selection block to proposal snapshot if missing.
Preserve model_selection in last_proposal.
acceptance_criteria: Goal with ananta_ollama_local uses ananta-worker routing and ananta-default:latest model where applicable.
Goal with opencode_ollama_local uses opencode routing and local Ollama target provider.
The chosen provider/model/backend is visible in last_proposal and LLM profile.
Tests verify selection is not only persisted but consumed.

tasks.title: ananta-default.Modelfile Basis korrigieren oder dokumentieren
tasks.description: The current Modelfile uses FROM ananta-default. Verify whether this is intentional or recursive/problematic in Ollama.
implementation_notes: If ananta-default is meant to derive from an existing base model, set FROM to that real base model.
If it is intentionally self-referential due to external import state, document why and add a smoke check.
Avoid hidden broken model import behavior.
acceptance_criteria: ollama create/import for ananta-default works from a clean environment.
There is no infinite/self-referential Modelfile dependency unless explicitly supported and tested.
Docs explain how ananta-default:latest is created.
Acceptance runner can verify required model availability before scenario start.
verification: Add or update a smoke test if Ollama is available in CI/local compose.

tracks.title: LLM-Profiling und Observability trennscharf machen
tracks.rationale: Synthetic entries prove orchestration activity but must never be confused with real provider calls. Token/latency statistics must remain trustworthy.

tasks.title: llm_call_profile Schema formalisieren
tasks.description: Define a stable schema for llm_call_profile entries shared by real LLM integration, worker strategies and synthetic fallback.
implementation_notes: Fields: name, backend, provider, model, success, latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens, source, estimated, error_type, error_message, started_at, ended_at.
Use one builder/normalizer where possible.
Reject malformed profile entries in tests or normalize them deterministically.
acceptance_criteria: All llm_call_profile entries have the same documented field set.
Real entries have estimated=false and source identifying real invocation layer.
Synthetic entries have estimated=true and source=orchestrator_synthetic.
Tests cover real, synthetic and malformed profile payloads.

tasks.title: Synthetic Metrics aus echten Statistiken ausschließen
tasks.description: Ensure synthetic LLM profile entries are used only as orchestration evidence, not as real latency/token/cost data.
implementation_notes: All aggregations must separate calls_seen_real, calls_seen_synthetic, calls_seen_estimated.
Real latency/token averages must ignore estimated=true.
Acceptance reports must show both categories explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: A report with only synthetic entries shows real call count 0.
Synthetic entries do not increase real token totals or latency averages.
Acceptance report still records propose activity based on synthetic entries.
Tests assert this separation.

tasks.title: Provider response usage normalization erweitern
tasks.description: Normalize token usage for Ollama, OpenAI-compatible APIs, Anthropic-like payloads and LM Studio variants.
implementation_notes: Cover prompt_tokens/completion_tokens/total_tokens.
Cover input_tokens/output_tokens.
Cover prompt_eval_count/eval_count.
Never throw on unknown provider payloads. Return empty usage instead.
acceptance_criteria: Token usage extraction works for at least three provider response shapes.
Unknown/malformed usage returns empty dict without exception.
total_tokens is calculated if missing but prompt/completion exist.
Tests include malformed and partial usage payloads.

tasks.title: LLM-Profil in last_proposal dauerhaft persistieren
tasks.description: Ensure the LLM profile survives snapshot merge, task state transitions and later proposal updates.
implementation_notes: Check _merged_last_proposal_snapshot behavior.
Do not overwrite existing real profile with synthetic fallback.
Prefer real profile when available.
acceptance_criteria: Existing real llm_call_profile is preserved after merge.
Synthetic fallback is only added when no real/preferred profile exists and fallback is allowed.
Task detail API exposes last_proposal with llm_call_profile.
Regression tests cover overwrite prevention.

tracks.title: Provider Observer produktionsreif machen
tracks.rationale: The hub now probes Ollama and LM Studio directly. This must be safe, cache-aware, observable and non-blocking.

tasks.title: Provider Observer API-Endpunkt bereitstellen
tasks.description: Expose provider observer snapshots through a stable authenticated system endpoint.
implementation_notes: Endpoint should support optional force_refresh for admins only.
Return enabled, source, providers, observed_at, ttl_seconds, timeout_seconds.
Do not include credentials or raw authorization headers.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated user can read provider observer snapshot.
force_refresh requires admin or is rejected deterministically.
Disabled observer returns enabled=false without probing providers.
Tests cover enabled, disabled and cache-hit behavior.

tasks.title: Provider-Probes strikt timeout-sicher machen
tasks.description: Provider probing must never block the hub/autopilot loop for too long.
implementation_notes: Clamp timeout to configured min/max.
Handle connection refused, DNS failures, timeout and malformed JSON.
Return status instead of throwing to caller.
acceptance_criteria: A dead provider returns ok=false with stable status.
Probe timeout is clamped to the allowed range.
No provider probe exception escapes snapshot().
Tests simulate failed provider calls.

tasks.title: Provider Observer in Acceptance Runner aufnehmen
tasks.description: Acceptance reports should include provider observer snapshots before and after each scenario.
implementation_notes: Capture pre_run_provider_snapshot and post_run_provider_snapshot.
Use observer to distinguish provider unavailable vs autopilot failure.
Keep reports redacted.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance report includes provider observer state per scenario.
Provider unavailable condition is reported as its own criterion or diagnostic.
Report does not fail with traceback when observer endpoint is unavailable; it records diagnostic fallback.
verification: Run scripts/first_goal_acceptance_runner.py against local compose with Ollama down and up.

tracks.title: Autopilot Planning Recovery kontrolliert und deterministisch machen
tracks.rationale: Automatic recovery from stalled planning is useful but can hide underlying bugs if not bounded, visible and tested.

tasks.title: Planning-Recovery Status sichtbar machen
tasks.description: Expose planning_recovery attempts and last error/reason in goal detail/readiness diagnostics.
implementation_notes: Keep data in execution_preferences.planning_recovery or normalized diagnostics field.
Do not expose internal tracebacks to normal users.
Admin can see more detailed recovery diagnostics if supported.
acceptance_criteria: Goal detail shows recovery attempt count and last_reason when recovery happened.
Recovery attempts are capped and visible.
Failed recovery transitions goal to failed with deterministic reason.
Tests cover no-tasks recovery and capped attempts.

tasks.title: Recovery nicht nur beim Detail-Endpoint triggern
tasks.description: Currently recovery can be coupled to fetching goal detail. Move or duplicate the recovery trigger into the autopilot/tick path so UI polling is not required.
implementation_notes: Create a service-level method recover_stalled_planning_goal(goal).
Call it from goal detail for compatibility and from autopilot tick for autonomous behavior.
Ensure it is idempotent and rate-limited.
acceptance_criteria: A stalled planning goal can recover without a UI/user calling /goals/<id>/detail.
Calling detail endpoint does not cause duplicate task creation.
Recovery service is unit-tested independent from Flask route.

tasks.title: No-Candidate und Blocked-By-Dependency Diagnosen verbessern
tasks.description: Make autopilot no-candidate states and blocked dependency states explainable in diagnostics.
implementation_notes: Record counts of no_candidates ticks by reason.
Differentiate no tasks, all blocked, policy blocked, provider unavailable, worker unavailable.
Surface summarized reason in diagnostics artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics can distinguish idle from deadlock from policy block.
Acceptance runner reports no-candidate stretches with reason.
Tests cover blocked_by_dependency and no executable step cases.
verification: Run scripts/goal_latency_diagnostics.py on a stalled run.

tasks.title: Autopilot Circuit Breaker im Acceptance Runner härten
tasks.description: The acceptance runner checks circuit breaker open_count. Make this criterion configurable and more diagnostic.
implementation_notes: Expose max_circuit_breaker_open_seconds option.
Report open breaker names if available.
Avoid hard-coded magic 120 seconds without CLI/config documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance runner CLI can configure circuit breaker tolerance.
Report includes open_count and optionally breaker names/durations.
Default behavior remains backward-compatible.

tracks.title: Acceptance Runner und Diagnostik systematisch machen
tracks.rationale: The acceptance runner became central for proving end-to-end behavior. It should be scenario-driven, reproducible and CI-friendly.

tasks.title: Scenario-Konfiguration aus JSON laden
tasks.description: Move hard-coded acceptance scenarios into a JSON scenario file.
implementation_notes: Define scenarios with id, label, goal_text, config_mode, config_profile, config_overrides, expected_outputs and timeouts.
Provide a default scenario file under scripts or tests/fixtures.
CLI accepts --scenario-file.
acceptance_criteria: Runner works with default built-in scenarios and external scenario file.
Invalid scenario file fails before running with clear error.
Scenario id and label are included in reports.
Tests parse valid and invalid scenario files.

tasks.title: Acceptance-Kriterien versionieren
tasks.description: Give acceptance reports an explicit schema_version and stable criterion ids/names.
implementation_notes: Add report_schema_version.
Define stable criterion ids such as goal_ingestion, task_materialization, autopilot_takeover.
Keep numeric ids only as display order if desired.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance reports include schema_version.
Criterion ids are stable strings, not only numeric positions.
A JSON schema validates generated reports.
Existing report readers are either updated or backward compatible.
verification: Generate report and validate it against schema.

tasks.title: Runner DB-Reset optional und sicher machen
tasks.description: The runner contains destructive DB reset behavior. Make it opt-in, environment-guarded and impossible against non-local/prod by accident.
implementation_notes: Require --reset-db plus --i-understand-this-deletes-local-test-data or equivalent.
Check base_url and container name against local allowlist.
Print/report reset action without exposing credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Runner never resets DB unless explicit destructive flags are present.
Runner refuses reset against non-local base_url by default.
Reset target container/db are configurable but guarded.
Help text warns clearly.
verification: Manual dry run without reset flags.

tasks.title: Latency Diagnostics mit Ursachen anreichern
tasks.description: The latency diagnostics currently show phase timings. Add reason breakdown for idle and blocked time.
implementation_notes: Collect queued_to_assigned, assigned_to_propose_done, propose_to_execute_done, execute_to_terminal as before.
Add reason categories from task/autopilot state if available.
Separate LLM wait, provider unavailable, worker unavailable, dependency blocked, policy blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics include phase timings and reason_breakdown_seconds.
No available reason is represented as unknown, not silently omitted.
Synthetic LLM profile is excluded from real latency stats.
verification: Run diagnostics on at least one successful and one stalled sample run.

tasks.title: Acceptance Runner CI-Modus einführen
tasks.description: Provide a CI-safe mode that runs only deterministic local tests and skips provider-dependent live checks unless explicitly enabled.
implementation_notes: CLI flag --ci-safe or --live-provider-checks=false.
Live provider checks can be marked skipped rather than failed.
Return non-zero only for required deterministic criteria.
acceptance_criteria: CI mode can run without Ollama/LM Studio installed.
Skipped live checks are visible in the report.
Local unit/integration checks still fail correctly on regressions.
verification: Run runner in CI-safe mode on a machine without Ollama.

tracks.title: Repo von Runtime-Artefakten und Workspace-Müll trennen
tracks.rationale: The commit range added many generated project-workspaces and artifact JSON files. This can pollute history and confuse source-vs-output boundaries.

tasks.title: Generated Artifacts klassifizieren
tasks.description: Decide which generated artifacts are fixtures and which should be ignored/removed from source control.
implementation_notes: Classify each committed artifact/workspace file as source, fixture, sample report or runtime output.
Move intentional fixtures to tests/fixtures with small stable content.
Remove accidental runtime outputs from repo in a dedicated cleanup commit.
acceptance_criteria: Every committed file under artifacts/ and project-workspaces/ has a documented reason or is removed.
.gitignore prevents new runtime workspace output from being committed by default.
Tests using sample artifacts reference tests/fixtures, not live runtime folders.
Repo status after a local acceptance run does not show generated files unless explicitly requested.
verification: Run acceptance runner locally, then git status must not show generated output by default.

tasks.title: .gitignore für Runtime-Ausgaben erweitern
tasks.description: Ignore generated workspaces and acceptance/diagnostic outputs by default, while allowing explicit fixtures.
implementation_notes: Ignore project-workspaces/** by default unless a .keep or fixtures subfolder is used.
Ignore artifacts/*.json generated by runners unless under artifacts/fixtures or tests/fixtures.
Document how to intentionally commit a fixture.
acceptance_criteria: New project-workspaces/* files are ignored by git.
New acceptance report JSON files are ignored unless placed in fixture path.
Existing intentional fixtures remain tracked.

tasks.title: Sample Reports minimieren
tasks.description: Replace many full runtime reports with a minimal set of stable test fixtures.
implementation_notes: Keep one success fixture, one provider-unavailable fixture, one stalled/deadlock fixture.
Strip volatile timestamps and ids where possible.
Update tests to load fixtures from tests/fixtures/reports.
acceptance_criteria: No large or duplicated runtime reports remain in root artifacts unless explicitly justified.
Fixture reports are deterministic and small.
Diagnostics tests pass against fixture reports.

tracks.title: Dokumentation und UI/API-Vertrag nachziehen
tracks.rationale: Backend behavior changed significantly. UI/CLI users need to see selected profile, effective config, provider state and real vs synthetic metrics.

tasks.title: Goal-Scoped Config Dokumentation schreiben
tasks.description: Document how goal-scoped config works and how profiles/overrides are resolved.
implementation_notes: Explain resolution order: system_default -> profile -> goal -> task.
Explain provenance, checksum/hash and redaction.
Show example payloads for config_profile and config_overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include at least one opencode_ollama_local example.
Docs explain that snapshots are immutable per goal after creation.
Docs explain which config keys are allowed and how unknown keys behave.

tasks.title: Frontend Contract für effective-config definieren
tasks.description: Define the frontend contract for displaying effective config snapshots.
implementation_notes: Document endpoint response fields.
Clearly mark redacted values.
Define UI labels for provenance sources: system_default, profile, goal, task.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend can render profile id, checksum, field provenance and redaction summary from documented response.
Contract distinguishes config_checksum and config_snapshot_hash or consolidates them if redundant.
Docs include example response.
verification: Manual compare docs vs actual endpoint response.

tasks.title: LLM Observability UI-Konzept festlegen
tasks.description: Define how UI should display real vs synthetic LLM profile information.
implementation_notes: Real provider call: show provider, model, latency, tokens, success/failure.
Synthetic orchestration marker: show as estimated/planning evidence, not as provider call.
Avoid mixing synthetic into cost or token charts.
acceptance_criteria: Docs define labels for estimated/synthetic entries.
Docs define which metrics are allowed in charts.
Docs warn against treating synthetic entries as model performance.

tracks.title: Regression-Testmatrix vervollständigen
tracks.rationale: The new behavior touches config, planning, workers, providers and reports. A matrix prevents regressions across combinations.

tasks.title: Goal Config Resolver Testmatrix erweitern
tasks.description: Add table-driven tests for resolution order and provenance.
implementation_notes: Cases: system only, profile only, goal override, task override, nested merge, unknown key, secret redaction.
Assert final config and field_sources exactly.
acceptance_criteria: Resolution order is proven by tests.
Nested dict merge behavior is proven by tests.
Unknown key behavior is proven by tests.
Secret redaction behavior is proven by tests.

tasks.title: End-to-End Goal Creation Matrix testen
tasks.description: Test goal creation with generic mode, new_software_project mode, each config profile and explicit overrides.
implementation_notes: Assert persisted execution_preferences contains snapshot, checksum and redaction summary.
Assert effective-config endpoint returns same snapshot.
Assert mode_data and execution contract still attach correctly.
acceptance_criteria: All default profiles work in goal creation tests.
Mode-specific goal creation still works with config_profile.
Invalid config blocks creation before planning starts.

tasks.title: Propose Strategy Profile Propagation testen
tasks.description: Verify profile/model metadata survives strategy normalization and fallback handling.
implementation_notes: Test strict JSON tool_calling output with metadata.
Test flexible normalization output with llm_call_profile.
Test malformed LLM response fallback keeps diagnostic profile.
acceptance_criteria: All propose strategies emit or preserve llm_call_profile where applicable.
Malformed response diagnostics include provider/model if known.
No strategy silently drops real LLM profile metadata.

tasks.title: Acceptance Runner Unit Tests ergänzen
tasks.description: Add unit tests for report generation, criteria evaluation and scenario parsing without requiring live services.
implementation_notes: Mock HTTP calls or isolate pure functions.
Extract criteria evaluation helpers if needed.
Do not require Docker or real Ananta service for unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests validate pass/fail criteria logic.
Unit tests validate scenario loading.
Unit tests validate report serialization.
Live E2E tests remain separate.

tracks.title: Commit-Hygiene und Entwicklungsprozess verbessern
tracks.rationale: Many commits are named 'fixup planning', making future review and bisecting unnecessarily hard.

tasks.title: Commit Message Policy dokumentieren
tasks.description: Define meaningful commit message conventions for future agent/human commits.
implementation_notes: Use prefixes like feat:, fix:, test:, docs:, refactor:, chore:.
Require component scope where useful: feat(goal-config): ...
Ban repeated generic messages like fixup planning except for local squashed history.
acceptance_criteria: CONTRIBUTING.md documents commit style.
AGENTS.md instructs agents to use meaningful commit messages.
Future TODOs reference this policy.

tasks.title: Agent Output Boundary dokumentieren
tasks.description: Agents must know which outputs belong into source and which into runtime/output folders only.
implementation_notes: Document that generated workspaces are normally not committed.
Document fixture exception path.
Document acceptance report handling.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md states that runtime artifacts are not source by default.
Fixture exception is explicit and narrow.
This policy aligns with .gitignore.
verification: Manual doc review plus git status after sample run.

global_acceptance_criteria: All new and existing tests for goal-scoped config, provider observer, LLM profiling, task scoped propose and acceptance runner pass locally.
Creating a goal with config_profile=opencode_ollama_local persists a redacted immutable config_snapshot with provenance and checksum.
Creating a goal with invalid config_overrides fails before a GoalDB record is persisted.
LLM call profiles clearly distinguish real provider calls from synthetic orchestration fallback entries.
Synthetic profile entries do not affect real latency, token or cost statistics.
Provider observer failures are reported as diagnostics, not uncaught exceptions.
Acceptance runner can run in CI-safe mode without live Ollama or LM Studio.
Runtime-generated project-workspaces and acceptance artifacts are ignored by default or moved to stable fixture paths.
The ananta-default Modelfile can be created/imported from a clean environment or is explicitly documented if it requires pre-existing state.
Documentation explains goal-scoped config resolution order, redaction, effective-config endpoint, profile selection and observability semantics.
notes: This TODO intentionally treats deterministic behavior and explicit diagnostics as higher priority than convenient silent fallback.
Some tasks may be split into smaller commits, but each task should keep its acceptance criteria intact.
The runtime artifact cleanup should be done carefully to avoid deleting intentional test fixtures.
